name: Archive Scribble Font
classification_notes: A casual handwritten script imitating spontaneous pen and pencil marks, with organic strokes and deliberate imperfections consistent with informal handwriting typefaces.
summary: A casual handwritten typeface imitating spontaneous pen sketches, offered in two subfamilies including an extras set.
description: Modelled on informal pen and pencil marks, the typeface leans into organic strokes and deliberate imperfections to suggest personal, unpolished writing. It ships in two subfamilies, Archive Scribble and Archive Scribble Extras, and is available in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats. Aimed at branding, packaging, social media and editorial contexts where an unstudied, hand-drawn quality is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Archive Scribble as a natural handwritten font inspired by authentic pen sketches and spontaneous handwriting, with organic strokes and expressive imperfections that capture the raw quality of notes, doodles and personal writing. They suggest it for branding, posters, journals, social media and packaging projects requiring a warm, human touch.

name: Bukbon
classification_notes: A bold, rounded display face with thick strokes and soft curves, designed explicitly for decorative and expressive use rather than text setting.
summary: A thick, rounded display face with a cheerful tone aimed at children's content, packaging and social media work.
description: Bukbon pairs heavy strokes with soft, smooth curves to produce a warm, approachable letterform suited to playful branding and promotional material. A slanted variant is included alongside the upright, giving two styles in total. The family covers OTF, TTF and WOFF formats but offers no weight range beyond its single bold cut.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Bukbon as a bold and playful display font with soft, rounded letterforms and a cheerful, friendly personality. They recommend it for branding, logos, packaging, posters, children's projects and social media where a fun, eye-catching and approachable style is needed.

name: Serunya Ramadhan
classification_notes: A bold Latin display face with Arabic-inspired letterforms; classified as display due to its decorative character and suitability exclusively for headlines and short-form use rather than body text.
summary: A bold Latin display font drawing on Arabic calligraphic forms, aimed at Ramadan and Eid-themed design work.
description: The letterforms take visual cues from Arabic calligraphy whilst remaining Latin in structure, giving it a festive, culturally specific character suited to posters, packaging and celebratory branding. An extrude variant adds a dimensional option for headlines. Alternate characters and stylistic sets provide layout flexibility within a single weight offering.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Serunya Ramadhan as a bold Latin display font with an Arabic-inspired style that feels strong, modern and unique. They note it is packed with alternate characters, stylistic sets and shape variations, and recommend it for headlines, logos, posters, packaging and any project requiring a bold and distinctive appearance.

name: Girtyne – Modern Sans Family
classification_notes: The foundry describes it as a modern classic sans serif balancing clean geometry with humanist touches, placing it at the intersection of geometric and humanist sans traditions.
summary: A five-weight modern sans with variable font support and Cyrillic coverage, aimed at branding, editorial and UI work.
description: Girtyne sits between geometric precision and humanist warmth, offering five weights from Extra Light to Semi Bold alongside a fully variable font file. Cyrillic support extends its usefulness beyond standard Latin-only packages. The family targets branding, packaging, editorial layouts and interface design where a clean, confident sans is needed without strong stylistic eccentricity.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Girtyne as a refined modern classic sans serif designed for clarity, elegance and versatility, balancing clean geometry with humanist touches. Available in five weights and as a variable font, it includes an extended character set with full Cyrillic support, making it suitable for branding, editorial layouts, UI design, packaging and display use.

name: Mochi Boom
classification_notes: Soft, inflated letterforms with rounded terminals place this firmly in the bubble/rounded display category, designed for decorative headline use rather than text setting.
summary: A bubbly rounded display font with Latin and partial Japanese script support, aimed at food packaging, social media and children's branding.
description: Soft, inflated letterforms take direct cues from the texture of mochi, giving the typeface a rounded, friendly quality suited to food brands, cafes and merchandise packaging. Two subfamilies are included: the standard cut and an extruded variant for added dimensionality. Latin letters are joined by Hiragana and Katakana, though Youon combined characters are not supported.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Mochi Boom as a playful display font with soft rounded shapes inspired by the chewy charm of mochi, suited to food brands, packaging, posters and social media graphics. It supports Latin alongside Japanese Hiragana and Katakana, and is available in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats.

name: AOT Creamilk
classification_notes: A condensed display sans with decorative style variants including rounded, slanted and extruded cuts, clearly intended for headlines and visual branding rather than text setting.
summary: A condensed display sans offered in eight style variants spanning rounded, slanted and extruded treatments for bold headline work.
description: Eight styles built around a single condensed uppercase-led structure, with variants that mix rounded corners, slanted angles and extruded depth to expand the tonal range from clean to expressive. The family suits poster design, logo work and visual branding where layering or stylistic contrast is required. Multilingual Latin coverage across a broad range of languages is included as standard.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe AOT Creamilk as a condensed display font with serious attitude, combining clean lines with a playful spirit. The eight styles, including Regular, Rounded, Slant and Extrude, are designed to give full creative control across bold headlines, layered typography and expressive visual branding.

name: Ombre Nouveau
classification_notes: A display serif with retro styling, positioned for headlines, branding and editorial use rather than continuous text. The name and aesthetic reference Art Nouveau influences blended with contemporary proportions.
summary: A retro-inflected display serif family spanning eight weights with matching italics and a variable font file, aimed at editorial and branding work.
description: Eight weights from Thin to Extra Bold, each with an italic companion, give the family reasonable range for headline and identity use. A variable font file is included alongside OTF, TTF and WOFF formats, allowing continuous weight interpolation. The styling leans on Art Nouveau references and vintage warmth without committing fully to either period aesthetic.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Ombre Nouveau as a stylish serif family that blends retro charm with contemporary elegance, crafted for editorial layouts, branding, logos and sophisticated headlines. They highlight the variable font file for fine-tuning weight and style, and note coverage of a wide range of Latin-based languages with ligature support included.

name: AOT Serial Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced typeface with a clean, contemporary structure throughout all five weights; sits firmly in the mono category with no proportional variant offered.
summary: A five-weight monospaced sans from Prioritype Co, released with a variable font file and aimed at code, UI and editorial work.
description: Released in April 2025, it spans Light to Bold with each weight available as OTF, TTF, WOFF and a variable font file. The design keeps a clean geometric structure with enough personality to move beyond purely functional code typography into branding and poster work. There are no italics or condensed cuts, which limits range but keeps the offering focused.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe AOT Serial Mono as a modern monospaced typeface with a stylish, contemporary edge, built for precision and personality. They pitch it across code, branding, editorial layouts and digital interfaces, noting that the five weights and variable font file suit everything from futuristic UI design to minimal poster work.

name: AOT Lost Contact
classification_notes: A pixel-grid display typeface with monospaced proportions, designed for screen-forward contexts such as gaming interfaces and tech branding rather than continuous text setting.
summary: A two-style pixel typeface offering a standard and rounded cut, aimed at gaming interfaces, tech branding and retro-inflected digital work.
description: AOT Lost Contact pairs a crisp square pixel style with a softened rounded variant, giving designers a choice between hard-edged and approachable treatments within the same visual language. The monospaced construction keeps letterforms orderly and consistent, which suits UI elements, game menus and arcade-styled typography. Available in OTF, TTF and WOFF, it covers the practical bases for both screen and print use.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe AOT Lost Contact as a pixel font that blends retro charm with modern readability, inspired by monospaced typefaces. They position it for gaming interfaces, pixel art, tech branding, UI/UX design and futuristic editorial layouts, and highlight its two styles, Regular and Rounded, as offering flexibility in creative expression.

name: Detroit Comic
classification_notes: A comic-book lettering family drawing on classic American comic strip typography, combining bold headline styles with handwritten speech-bubble variants across ten decorative styles.
summary: A ten-style comic lettering family from Prioritype, pairing bold headline cuts with handwritten speech-bubble variants for illustrated and children's work.
description: The family splits into three distinct branches: Detroit Comic for high-impact titles, Detroit Hop for a looser, more energetic alternative, and Detroit Hand for speech bubbles and captions with italic and bold cuts. Each headline branch ships with shadow and outline layering variants, giving designers pre-built depth effects without manual stacking. The pairing between the headline and hand styles is designed to hold together across posters, social media, comics and children's publishing.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Detroit Comic as a bold, playful and dynamic family inspired by classic comic lettering but refined for a smoother, more modern look. They highlight the ten unique styles as offering endless creative possibilities, and emphasise the pairing between the Detroit Comic headline fonts and the Detroit Hand styles as allowing professional, engaging results across comics, branding, children's books and digital content.

name: PC Merchis
classification_notes: A bold, all-caps-oriented display typeface spanning five widths from Condensed to Expanded, designed specifically for poster and headline use rather than running text.
summary: A bold display typeface offered in five widths from Condensed to Expanded, with variable font support, aimed at posters, branding and editorial headlines.
description: Released in February 2025, PC Merchis spans five width variants from Condensed to Expanded, available as OTF, TTF, WOFF and variable font files. The design targets poster and headline work where a strong silhouette is required, and the width range gives designers flexibility across tight and open compositions. Latin Extended language coverage includes over 60 languages across African, European and Asian Latin scripts.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe PC Merchis as a bold display font designed for posters with a strong, impactful presence. Featuring five widths from Condensed to Expanded, it is positioned for branding, headlines and statement designs, with multilingual support and availability in variable font format.

name: Qurova
classification_notes: Described as a modern sans-serif with clean lines and geometric sensibility, positioned for branding and logo work with a full weight range.
summary: A geometric sans-serif with five weights, aimed at logo and branding work across a wide range of Latin languages.
description: Clean-lined and upright, it covers Light through Bold with no italic cuts, supplied in OTF, TTF, WOFF and variable formats. Language support spans over sixty Latin-script languages including Swahili, Norwegian and Uzbek. Positioned squarely at identity and logo work, though the design offers little to distinguish it from the broader market of similarly styled branding typefaces.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Qurova as a modern sans-serif typeface crafted for professional branding and corporate design, combining clean lines with versatile functionality across a full weight range, suited to logos, visual identities and sophisticated branding needs.

name: Primal Sailor
classification_notes: A condensed, high-contrast display serif designed primarily for headlines and branding at large sizes, with sharp serif details and bold proportions placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A single-weight condensed display serif with sharp detailing, aimed at posters, logos and headline work.
description: Sharp serif details and bold condensed proportions give this typeface a commanding presence at large sizes. It ships with alternates and ligatures alongside broad Latin language coverage spanning over sixty languages. A practical option for branding and print work that calls for a strong vertical rhythm without much typographic variety in weight.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Primal Sailor as a condensed display serif with a bold, striking and commanding presence. They note its sleek yet prominent letterforms blend modern aesthetics with timeless appeal, and position it for posters, branding, logos and creative projects requiring a dynamic and authoritative look. The font includes uppercase, lowercase, numerals, punctuation, alternates, ligatures and multilingual support, supplied in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats.

name: Kolosal Display
classification_notes: Tagged and styled as a display serif with bold, attention-commanding letterforms and a companion italic; the page references poster and branding use cases typical of display serifs.
summary: A bold display serif in two styles — upright and italic — aimed at posters, branding and fashion-adjacent work.
description: Kolosal Display offers a single weight in regular and italic cuts, with the upright positioned as heavy and commanding and the italic as a lighter, more refined companion. Language coverage is broad, spanning Latin-script languages across Europe and Africa. The overall offering is thin — one weight, two styles — and the design sits in generic display serif territory without a particularly distinctive voice.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Kolosal Display as a bold and memorable typeface designed to make a statement, available in two styles: a thick, powerful regular and a sleek italic. They position it for eye-catching posters and branding designs, citing a modern yet timeless appeal, with multilingual Latin support and files supplied in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats.

name: Nivillia – Bold Script Font
classification_notes: A bold connected script with smooth curves and flowing letterforms, typical of contemporary brush or pen-style script typefaces used in branding and logotype work.
summary: A bold connected script with flowing curves, aimed at logos, packaging and branding work across a wide range of Latin-script languages.
description: Nivillia is a single-weight bold script that leans on smooth, stylised letterforms suited to logotypes, invitations and product packaging. The font ships with alternates, ligatures and a discretionary underline system accessed via underscore combinations, giving designers some flexibility within a single style. Language coverage is broad, spanning over sixty Latin-script languages from Afrikaans to Zulu.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Nivillia as a bold script font combining elegance and charm, suited to logos, branding and creative designs. They highlight its smooth curves and stylish flow as lending a sense of class to both modern and timeless aesthetics, and note support for multilingual Latin characters alongside alternates and ligatures.

name: Heuvel Grotesk
classification_notes: A contemporary grotesque sans with inktraps and a variable font file, positioned squarely in the modern grotesque tradition with a broad weight range and matching italics.
summary: A contemporary grotesque with inktraps across eight weights, available as a variable font with matching italics throughout.
description: Sixteen styles span ExtraLight to Black with corresponding italics, and the family ships as a variable font file alongside static OTF, TTF and WOFF formats. Inktraps add a subtle typographic detail that lifts it slightly above the average generic grotesque. Latin Extended coverage across a wide range of languages makes it suitable for multilingual branding and web projects.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Heuvel Grotesk as a versatile sans serif blending classic aesthetics with a modern touch, featuring striking inktraps and offered in 8 weights plus italics totalling 16 styles, with a variable font file included. They recommend it for branding, websites, headlines and logo design, with multilingual support across a broad range of Latin-script languages.

name: Super Parody – Retro Display Sans
classification_notes: A bold display sans with retro styling and high contrast details, available in regular and slanted cuts, designed for headlines and branding rather than text setting.
summary: A bold retro display sans in two styles, Regular and Slant, aimed at poster, branding and headline work with a vintage feel.
description: Super Parody offers two cuts, Regular and Slant, both built around chunky letterforms with contrasting strokes that nod to display type from the 1960s through to the 1990s. The character set covers uppercase, lowercase, numerals and punctuation alongside a wide range of Latin-based languages. Suited to posters, merchandise and social media graphics where a retro aesthetic is the priority.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Super Parody as a bold display font that blends retro charm with a modern edge, offering two versatile styles suited to branding, posters and striking headlines, with multilingual support across a wide range of Latin-based languages.

name: Erotic Display
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a display font with sensuous curves and striking lines aimed at posters, branding and magazines, placing it firmly in the fashion display category.
summary: A fashion-oriented display face from Prioritype Co, trading on curves and editorial flair for poster and branding work.
description: Built for high-contrast, attention-seeking layouts, the design leans on rounded, sensuous letterforms suited to cosmetics, fashion editorial and premium branding. It ships in a single weight with OTF, TTF and WOFF files. The aesthetic sits somewhere between retro glamour and contemporary minimalism, though the execution is fairly generic within its niche.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype Co describe Erotic Display as crafted for bold, expressive designs that capture attention and ignite intrigue. They cite its sensuous curves and striking lines as making it ideal for posters, high-end branding and avant-garde magazines, blending elegance with an edgy allure for those wanting to make a statement.

name: Drink Cava
classification_notes: A bold display typeface with retro-modern character, combining chunky letterforms with slim contrasting details; sits firmly in the display category rather than a functional text sans.
summary: A bold display sans with retro-modern character, mixing chunky weight with slim detailing on select letterforms for poster and branding use.
description: Released in 2024 by Prioritype Co, Drink Cava pairs heavy weight with slim contrasting details on certain characters, giving it a distinctly retro-modern feel that draws on 60s through 90s graphic vernacular. The single bold weight covers uppercase, lowercase, numerals and punctuation, with multilingual support spanning a wide range of Latin-based languages. Best suited to posters, logotypes, merchandise and social media graphics where visual punch is the priority.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype Co describe Drink Cava as a bold font with slim details on certain characters, making it daring and unique. They position it as playful with a retro-modern vibe, recommending it for posters, branding and logos. Files are supplied in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats with multilingual Latin coverage.

name: PC Regtian
classification_notes: A bold display-weight sans-serif with rounded and standard variants, designed primarily for headlines, posters and branding rather than continuous text.
summary: A chunky display sans in two styles, Regular and Rounded, with retro and groovy leanings suited to posters and logotypes.
description: Released in September 2024, PC Regtian comes in Regular and Rounded cuts, both built around heavy, attention-seeking letterforms with alternate characters for added variety. The retro character references 1960s to 1990s vernacular typography, making it a candidate for music, merchandise and packaging work. Latin Extended language support covers a wide range of Western and African languages.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe PC Regtian as a bold, attention-grabbing display font with a dynamic edge, available in Regular and Rounded styles. They position it for posters, logos and branding projects, noting it suits both modern and retro-leaning design directions. It includes uppercase, lowercase, numerals, punctuation, alternates and broad multilingual support.

name: Super Taste – Bold Script Font
classification_notes: A thick, connected script with retro styling and bold strokes; the foundry explicitly classifies it as a bold script, and its vintage/groovy aesthetic places it firmly in the display script category.
summary: A thick, retro-inflected bold script available in Regular and an Extrude variant, aimed at branding and poster work with a vintage flavour.
description: Two styles make up the family: a standard bold script and an extruded version that adds dimensional depth for poster and packaging use. Characters are rounded and playful with ligatures and alternates included, and the language set covers a broad range of Latin-based scripts. The aesthetic sits somewhere between 1970s groovy lettering and contemporary retro branding, making it a competent but fairly generic option for that niche.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Super Taste as a bold script that blends modern flair with a retro touch, featuring playful yet structured characters suited to logos, branding, posters and promotional materials. Available in Regular and Extrude styles, it aims to evoke a vintage yet fresh vibe, with multilingual support and ligatures and alternates included.

name: Comparison Display Font
classification_notes: Classified as display due to its deliberate stylistic contrast between expanded uppercase and condensed lowercase letterforms, designed explicitly for headline and poster use rather than text setting.
summary: A single-weight display sans that pairs expanded uppercase letters with condensed lowercase for built-in stylistic contrast.
description: The typeface splits its case registers across two width extremes: uppercase characters are set in a broad, bold expanded style while lowercase sits in a tight condensed form. This contrast is the sole design conceit, intended for posters, headlines and logos where the mix-and-match width shift provides visual interest without additional styles. Language coverage is broad, spanning Latin-based European and African scripts.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Comparison Display Font as a dynamic mix-and-match typeface combining condensed and expanded sans-serif styles, with bold expansive uppercase letters contrasted against sleek condensed lowercase. They position it for posters, branding, headlines and logos, citing its balance of contrast and cohesion as its core appeal.

name: Aesthetic Wonder
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a modern serif; the naming conventions and styling suggest a high-contrast display serif in the contemporary fashion-adjacent mould, with variable font support across eight weights.
summary: A variable modern serif in eight weights aimed at branding, fashion and editorial work across a wide Latin language range.
description: Eight weights from ExtraLight to Black, available as a variable font alongside static OTF, TTF and WOFF files. Coverage spans a broad range of Latin-script languages including many African and European tongues. Positioned at fashion, cosmetic and lifestyle branding markets, though the design itself is fairly generic within that segment.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype present Aesthetic Wonder as an elegant modern serif that blends classic sophistication with a contemporary touch, offering eight crafted weights and variable font flexibility. They suggest it for branding, logos, posters and visual identity work, noting multilingual support and availability in OTF, TTF, WOFF and variable formats.

name: Barich Moderne Sans
classification_notes: A clean, modern sans-serif with geometric tendencies marketed for display and branding use, with no strong humanist or grotesque characteristics described.
summary: A four-weight geometric sans with variable font support, aimed at branding, poster and packaging work.
description: Released in July 2024 by Prioritype, this family covers Regular through Bold with a variable font file included alongside OTF, TTF and WOFF formats. Coverage extends across a broad range of Latin-based languages including European and African scripts. The design is generic in character, positioned squarely at logo and social media work rather than extended text setting.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Barich Moderne Sans as a charming and elegant font family suited to logo design, posters, magazines and websites, offering uppercase, lowercase, numerals, punctuation and wide multilingual support across OTF, TTF, Variable and WOFF formats.

name: Melted Gold – SVG Font
classification_notes: An OpenType SVG color font with photorealistic hand-painted texture, placing it firmly in the display category as a decorative, bitmap-embedded script style.
summary: A hand-painted OpenType SVG font with photorealistic molten gold texture, supplied with PNG letter sets and Photoshop Actions for colour-swapping.
description: Each letterform is painted by hand using gold paint, with the fluid texture embedded directly into the font as an OpenType SVG colour font. The package includes OTF, WOFF and PNG formats alongside Photoshop Actions designed to simplify colour changes. Suited to luxury packaging, social media graphics and poster work where a tactile, metallic effect is the primary requirement.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Melted Gold as crafted by hand using luxurious gold paint, with each letter capturing the fluid beauty and rich texture of molten metal. They position it for projects requiring timeless sophistication and grandeur, and bundle PNG letter sets and Photoshop Actions to make colour customisation straightforward.

name: Mellow Punk – Cute Kids Font
classification_notes: A casual, child-like handwriting typeface designed for decorative and display use, drawing on the aesthetic of elementary school penmanship exercises rather than formal script traditions.
summary: A child-handwriting-inspired display face in two weights, aimed at educational materials, children's books and playful branding.
description: Mellow Punk mimics the uneven, earnest penmanship of a child learning to write, with each character drawn to suggest the wobble and spontaneity of early school exercises. It ships in Regular and Bold weights with full uppercase, lowercase, numerals and punctuation, alongside OTF, TTF, WOFF, AI, EPS and SVG formats. Bonus graphics are included for use alongside the typeface in decorative layouts.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Mellow Punk as a whimsical font inspired by a child's first handwriting exercises, intended to bring a nostalgic, personal feel to educational materials, children's books, invitations and playful branding, with each character reflecting a sense of discovery and joy.

name: PC Magrok
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a freestyle contemporary display font; the slanted cuts, wide weight range and expressive letterforms place it firmly in the display category rather than functional text sans.
summary: A variable contemporary display sans in nine weights with matching slanted cuts, aimed at logo, poster and branding work.
description: PC Magrok spans nine weights from Thin to Black, each with a slanted companion, giving 18 static fonts alongside a variable file. Coverage extends across a broad range of Latin-script languages including Afrikaans, Swahili, Indonesian and Zulu. The freestyle construction suits headline and identity contexts where some personality is wanted without moving into script or decorative territory.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe PC Magrok as a freestyle contemporary display font available in 18 styles covering regular and slant variants, with variable and static files in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats. They recommend it for logo, branding and poster design, and note multilingual support across a wide range of Latin-based languages.

name: Divine Opulent – Display Serif
classification_notes: A decorative serif designed explicitly for display use, with mixed uppercase and lowercase letterforms as its primary aesthetic feature, placing it firmly in the display category rather than a functional text serif.
summary: A single-weight display serif with deliberately mismatched uppercase and lowercase letterforms, aimed at fashion branding and poster work.
description: The mixed-case aesthetic is the central conceit here: upper and lowercase letters are designed to contrast with each other, creating visual tension intended for logos, posters and cosmetic branding. Available in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats, it covers an extensive range of Latin-based languages. A narrow brief executed at a modest level of craft, best suited to decorative applications where texture matters more than typographic precision.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Divine Opulent as a serif display font that mixes and matches different uppercase and lowercase letters, with the aesthetic value lying in that contrast. They suggest it for posters, logos and branding work, and supply it with full multilingual Latin coverage including Afrikaans, French, German, Portuguese, Spanish, Swahili and many others.

name: Rich Versatile – Display Sans
classification_notes: Marketed explicitly as a display sans serif intended for headlines, branding and posters rather than continuous text, with a bold weight and retro-to-modern aesthetic range.
summary: A single-weight bold display sans aimed at branding, poster and logo work with a retro-to-modern tone.
description: Released by Prioritype Co in April 2024, this is a bold display sans covering uppercase, lowercase, numerals and punctuation across an extensive Latin-extended multilingual character set. It is offered in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats and targets headline, packaging and social media applications. The styling gestures at both retro and contemporary aesthetics without committing strongly to either.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype Co describe Rich Versatile as a bold sans serif display font designed to bring retro and modern design themes together, calling it well suited to branding, poster and logo work. The font includes multilingual support across a wide range of Latin-based languages and is available in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats.

name: The Hype Summer Groove Font Bundle
classification_notes: A bundle of 20 decorative display styles spanning retro, bubble, funky and groovy aesthetics; no single classification applies across the collection, but display is the dominant intent throughout.
summary: A bundle of 20 retro-inflected display fonts from Prioritype, spanning bubble lettering, blocky styles, outlines and extrude variants aimed at merchandise and social graphics.
description: Released in April 2024, the bundle collects 20 decorative styles under a single licence, covering groovy 70s curves, blocky extrude cuts, outlines and hand-drawn forms. The range is broad rather than deep, with most subfamilies offered as single weights or paired with a stylistic variant such as an outline or extrude version. Files ship in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats, making the bundle workable for print, web and basic branding applications.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe this as a collection of 13 unique and vibrant fonts adding retro flair and modern fun to design projects, offered at a 93% discount saving $228, and suitable for any creative endeavour from branding to merchandise.

name: Premier Font Vault Collection
classification_notes: The collection contains twelve typefaces spanning serifs, sans-serifs, scripts and display styles, making a single category classification impossible; display bundle is the most accurate umbrella term given the decorative, branding-oriented nature of the majority.
summary: A discounted bundle of twelve mixed-style display typefaces aimed at branding, logo and fashion design work.
description: Twelve typefaces sold together at a steep discount, covering decorative serifs, display scripts and assorted novelty styles. The collection is positioned at designers who want a broad toolkit for branding and print without spending heavily. Quality and consistency vary considerably across the included faces, and the bundle-discount framing is characteristic of volume-over-craft releases.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe the Premier Font Vault Collection as an unparalleled treasury of top-tier typography offered at a 98% discount, suitable for branding, web design and print media, with files available in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats for instant download.

name: Koreon Display
classification_notes: A bold stencil display typeface with large, attention-grabbing letterforms designed primarily for headlines, posters and branding rather than body text. The stencil construction and display intent place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A single-weight stencil display font with bold, blocky letterforms aimed at poster, branding and logo work.
description: A stencil display typeface from Prioritype with a retro-leaning character suited to posters, merchandise and logo lockups. It ships in OTF, TTF and WOFF formats and covers an extensive range of Latin-based languages including most Western and Eastern European variants. There is only one weight and no italic, so its application is narrow.
foundry_blurb: Prioritype describe Koreon Display as a striking stencil display font with bold and captivating letterforms, designed to stand out in branding, logos and posters. They position it as versatile and distinctive, suited to any design application requiring a bold statement.
